questions.question: What does the ICE framework stand for?
questions.explanation: ICE stands for Impact (how much will this move the needle), Confidence (how confident are you it will produce results), and Ease (how easy is it to execute). ICE Score = Impact x Confidence x Ease. Use it to prioritize sprint goals.

questions.question: Why does the sprint use a 30-day window instead of 90 days?

options.text: 90 days is too long for any business planning
30-day sprints create urgency and produce 3-5x more measurable results than vague quarterly goals
Most tools only have 30-day free trials

questions.explanation: 90-day plans are easy to postpone. 30-day sprints create urgency — you execute today instead of planning for tomorrow. Research shows structured 30-day sprints produce 3-5x more measurable results than vague quarterly goals.

questions.question: How many sprint goals should you select?
questions.explanation: Select your top 2-3 goals by ICE score. A primary goal, a secondary goal, and optionally a stretch goal. Trying to execute on more than 3 goals in 30 days dilutes focus and reduces the chance of completing any of them.

questions.question: A SMART sprint goal like 'do more outreach' is specific enough for a 30-day sprint.
questions.explanation: False. 'Do more outreach' is vague. A SMART sprint goal is measurable and time-bound: 'Send 120 cold emails per week for 4 weeks, targeting VP Sales at Series A SaaS companies, achieving 4%+ reply rate.' On Day 31 you can definitively say whether you hit it.

questions.question: Score your top 3 sprint goal candidates using the ICE framework. Which goals will you prioritize and why? What specific, measurable targets will you set for each?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response lists at least 3 goal candidates with ICE scores, selects 2-3 based on highest scores, and defines SMART targets (specific volume, measurable outcome, time-bound to 30 days) for each selected goal.
